task_completion.description: Open the form or section to add a new condition to the patient's chart.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Add Condition' form, modal, or page is visible within the OpenMRS interface.
The word 'Asthma' (case-insensitive) is typed into the condition name input field.

task_completion.description: Ensure the condition status is set to 'Active'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Active' status is selected via a radio button, dropdown menu, or is clearly indicated as the default status on the form.

task_completion.description: Submit the form to save the new condition.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The cursor is shown clicking a 'Save', 'Add', or 'Confirm' button to submit the condition form.

task_completion.description: Verify that 'Asthma' has been successfully added as an active condition.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The patient's dashboard or conditions list displays 'Asthma' and indicates its status is 'Active'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the OpenMRS web interface rather than using terminal commands or API calls.
integrity.visual_evidence: All actions take place within a standard web browser viewing the OpenMRS GUI. No terminal windows or scripts are used to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to manipulate the DOM and fake the condition.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser developer tools (Elements, Console, Network tabs) are not open at any point during the trajectory.

integrity.description: The agent added the condition to the patient chart that was initially displayed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The patient name and ID at the top of the chart remain consistent from the first screenshot to the last; the agent did not navigate away to a different patient.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The condition name must be 'Asthma' (case-insensitive) and its status must be explicitly set to or displayed as 'Active'.
